Peeler Environmental
Salisbury, NC · Pest Control
Live preview · built by
AID Marketing Agency
Missed call, 7:42pm tonight

Termites found.
Phone rings.
No one picks up.

Peeler Environmental has protected Rowan County homes and businesses since 1968 -- three generations of the Peeler family building the same reputation. But when a homeowner finds termite damage in their crawl space after hours and calls, that reputation does not help if the call goes to voicemail. Watch what happens instead.

Live on Peeler Environmental's actual number. Not a mockup, not a template with the name swapped in.
Nothing for Kip or the crew to install. Runs quietly behind the number you already give out.
Five-star reviews across multiple platforms, A+ BBB rated. This makes sure the next caller reaches you and becomes the next one.
Text thread · Peeler Environmental
Hi, I was down in the crawl space today and I found what looks like termite damage on some of the floor joists. Pretty worried about it. Do you all do inspections?7:42 PM
AI replied in 38 secondsThanks for reaching out to Peeler Environmental. Termite damage in a crawl space is something we deal with regularly across Rowan County. Can you describe what you are seeing -- mud tubes, hollow-sounding wood, or visible damage to the joists themselves?7:42 PM
There are some mud tubes along the foundation wall and the wood near one corner sounds hollow when I knock on it. Kind of panicking here.7:46 PM
Those are active signs and you are right to want it looked at fast. I can get a Peeler technician out for a full crawl space inspection Thursday morning or Friday afternoon. Which works? I will text you the technician's name and time to confirm.7:47 PM
First reply in 38 sec, no human touched a phone
Simulated exchange for demo purposes
Their own team, not a stock photo

A Peeler Environmental technician on a job in Rowan County. This is who answers the phone once it stops going to voicemail.

What one missed termite call can cost that homeowner
$7,500
average termite damage before it is even detected in NC homes

North Carolina's humid climate is a paradise for subterranean termites, and they work quietly. By the time a homeowner spots mud tubes in the crawl space, the average damage already runs $7,500 or more -- structural beam repair alone hits $2,000 to $10,000, foundation damage up to $25,000.

Here is the part that stings: 62 percent of calls to small businesses go unanswered, and 85 percent of voicemail-hitters never call back. They just find another inspector. This page catches the call before they do.

How the catch works

Three parts, no new number, nothing to install
01

The call gets missed, on purpose

Kip's team is out doing inspections across Rowan County. Nobody stops mid-job for a ringing phone. Termite calls spike on evenings and weekends, exactly when the office is closed and the technicians are already home.

Every unanswered call fires a text back in under a minute, while the homeowner still has Peeler on their screen instead of the next pest company on the Google list.

02

The AI asks the right questions

It knows termites and crawl spaces: mud tubes, hollow joists, moisture signs, how long since they noticed. Real qualifying, not a form.

03

An inspection gets booked

Day and time texted to the homeowner while they are still standing in that crawl space. Kip's crew shows up to a scheduled job, not a cold callback.

See it running on Peeler's number.

Fifteen minutes with Ryan. He will walk through exactly what you saw above, live, and give you a straight answer on whether it is worth doing for Peeler Environmental.

Book the walkthrough

No pitch deck, no obligation. Just a look at what this looks like on your phone.

"Whoever answers first gets the inspection. This is how Peeler answers first, every time."
